2605.06584.title: NeuroAgent: LLM Agents for Multimodal Neuroimaging Analysis and Research
2605.06584.paper_summary: Multimodal neuroimaging analysis often involves complex, modality-specific preprocessing workflows that require careful configuration, quality control, and coordination across heterogeneous toolchains. Beyond preprocessing, downstream statistical analysis and disease classification commonly require task-specific code, evaluation protocols, and data-format conventions, creating additional barriers between raw acquisitions and reproducible scientific analysis. We present NeuroAgent, an LLM-driven agentic framework that automates key preprocessing and analysis steps for heterogeneous neuroimaging data, including sMRI, fMRI, dMRI, and PET, and supports interactive downstream analysis through natural-language queries. NeuroAgent employs a hierarchical multi-agent architecture with a feedback-driven Generate-Execute-Validate engine: agents autonomously generate executable preprocessing code, detect and recover from runtime errors, and validate output integrity. We evaluate the system on 1,470 subjects pooled across all ADNI phases (CN=1,000, AD=470), where all subjects have sMRI and tabular data, with subsets also having Tau-PET (n=469), fMRI (n=278), and DTI ($n=620$). Pipeline ablation studies across multiple LLM backends show that capable models reach up to 100% intent-parsing accuracy, with the strongest backend (Qwen3.5-27B) reaching 84.8% end-to-end preprocessing step correctness. Automated recovery limits manual intervention to edge cases where human review is required via the Human-In-The-Loop interface. For Alzheimer's Disease classification using automatically preprocessed multimodal data, our agent ensemble achieves an AUC of 0.9518 with four modalities, outperforming all single-modality baselines. These results show that NeuroAgent can reduce the manual effort required for neuroimaging preprocessing and enable end-to-end automated analysis pipelines for neuroimaging research.
2605.06584.paper_summary_zh: 多模態神經影像分析通常涉及複雜的、特定模態的預處理工作流程，這些工作流程需要仔細配置、質量控制和跨異質工具鏈的協調。除了預處理之外，下游統計分析和疾病分類通常需要特定任務的代碼、評估協議和數據格式約定，這為原始數據的獲取和可重複的科學分析之間創造了額外的障礙。我們提出了NeuroAgent，一個由LLM驅動的代理框架，能自動化異質神經影像數據的關鍵預處理和分析步驟，包括sMRI、fMRI、dMRI和PET，並通過自然語言查詢支持互動式下游分析。NeuroAgent採用層次化的多代理架構，配備反饋驅動的生成-執行-驗證引擎：代理自動生成可執行的預處理代碼，檢測並從運行時錯誤中恢復，並驗證輸出完整性。我們在1,470名來自所有ADNI階段的受試者上評估該系統（CN=1,000，AD=470），所有受試者都有sMRI和表格數據，部分受試者還擁有Tau-PET（n=469）、fMRI（n=278）和DTI（$n=620$）。在多個LLM後端的管道消融研究中，顯示出能夠的模型達到高達100%的意圖解析準確率，最強的後端（Qwen3.5-27B）達到84.8%的端到端預處理步驟正確性。自動恢復將人工干預限制在需要人類審查的邊緣案例中，通過人機交互界面進行。對於使用自動預處理的多模態數據進行阿茲海默症分類，我們的代理集成在四種模態下達到0.9518的AUC，超越了所有單模態基準。這些結果顯示，NeuroAgent可以減少神經影像預處理所需的人工努力，並為神經影像研究啟用端到端的自動化分析管道。
2605.06584.author: Lujia Zhong et.al.
2605.06584.authors: Lujia Zhong, Yihao Xia, Jianwei Zhang, Shuo huang, Jiaxin Yue, Mingyang Xia, Yonggang Shi
